zh.Whitby, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾惠特比
zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Percy於大約1168年出生在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾惠特比時，她的父親Joscelin de Louvain 4th Baron de Percy49歲，母親Agnes de Percy35歲。她和John de Daiville I至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1235年在英國越可爾享年68歲。

zh-hans.Whitby, Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾惠特比

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Percy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Old French percehaie ‘pierce hedge’ (Old French percer ‘to pierce, penetrate’ + haie ‘hedge, fence’), perhaps with the sense of someone breaking into an enclosure. Percehaie is the name of one of the sons of the foxes Renart and Hermeline in the medieval French epic Roman de Renart, whose earliest known version is from the 1170s. The surname is older than that, but it may originate in a nickname for the fox as ‘enclosure piercer’, perhaps amounting to ‘chicken thief’.

habitational name from any of several places called Percy in Calvados, Eure, and Manche; William de Perci, the Domesday tenant-in-chief and under-tenant of Hugh, Earl of Chester, came from either Percy-en-Auge (Eure) or Percy (Manche).

variants.name: Piercey
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Percy  .

variants.name: Piercy
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Percy  .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Percy was born about 1168, in Whitby, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Joscelin de Louvain 4th Baron de Percy, was 49 and her mother, Agnes de Percy, was 35. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with John de Daiville I. She died in 1235, in Yorkshire, England, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Percy est née environ 1168, à Whitby, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Joscelin de Louvain 4th Baron de Percy, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Percy, avait 35 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec John de Daiville I. Elle est décédée en 1235, à Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Percy(이)가 대략 1168년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 휘트비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Joscelin de Louvain 4th Baron de Percy(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Percy(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 John de Daiville I(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1235년 영국 요르크실르에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.
